project.name: Pika
project.purpose: Pika helps groups rank choices fast through playful head-to-head matchups, then share the result.

contract.policy: Backend defines canonical v1 slice contract; frontend conforms after publication.

compliance.note: Any standards/compliance metadata tied to operating-health references is provisional until archivist verifies or restores the canonical reference.

topline.summary: The current Pika core flow is baseline-approved and locked for implementation. Engineering idle-risk is low because prototype completion is followed by concrete handoffs: backend deployment artifacts, frontend Vercel config/integration follow-through, and platform CI/readiness support. Operating-health standards-based monitoring remains provisional pending archivist verification of the canonical reference.
topline.decisionFocus: Execute the locked baseline flow through contract-backed integration, deployment/readiness artifacts, and explicit executive handoffs.

streams.name: engineering/build
streams.progress: backend complete; frontend prototype complete and baseline-locked; integration and deployment sequencing active
streams.summary: Backend MVP is complete and acknowledged. Frontend five-screen prototype is complete, pushed, and approved as the locked implementation baseline for the core flow, with concrete post-prototype handoffs already identified.
evidence: Frontend prototype complete and demoable; pushed with Playwright happy-path coverage.
CEO approved the current frontend prototype/core flow as Pika’s baseline product flow.
Concrete engineering follow-through is named: backend deployment artifacts, frontend Vercel config/integration, and platform CI support.
streams.nextMilestoneOrDecisionGate: Ship the next implementation handoff package: backend canonical/deployment artifacts, frontend contract wiring plus Vercel config, and platform CI support to move the locked flow toward deployable integration.
streams.next: Execute frontend-to-backend contract wiring against the locked baseline flow, then package deployment-facing artifacts.

streams.name: prototype clarity
streams.progress: core flow locked; future work narrowed to refinement after implementation progress
streams.summary: Prototype ambiguity is materially reduced: onboarding, option entry, matchup progression, and results reveal are locked at the flow level. Product ownership now shifts from reconsideration to a later refinement pass focused on polish, clarity, and comprehension.
evidence: CEO approved the current prototype/core flow as the baseline product direction.
Only polish-level UX/content adjustments are allowed unless a change alters onboarding, matchup logic, or results meaning.
Head-of-Product owns the next formal UX refinement pass after implementation catches up.
Head-of-Product directed brand to support a refinement brief, not a rethink.
streams.nextMilestoneOrDecisionGate: Run the post-implementation refinement pass for polish, clarity, and comprehension once integrated flow progress is visible.
streams.next: Defer major flow changes; prepare a refinement review after contract-backed implementation progress lands.

streams.name: launch/live monitoring
streams.progress: growth completed current deliverable; launch-facing follow-up remains downstream of integration
streams.summary: Growth has completed a current work package, while launch asset timing now depends more clearly on the engineering integration path and readiness handoffs than on unresolved product direction.
evidence: Growth is reflected as completed/pushed in current operating signals.
Brand consultation is now later-stage and limited to polish-level copy support unless product meaning changes.
Launch/live monitoring remains active as integration and packaging become clearer.
streams.nextMilestoneOrDecisionGate: Convert the locked flow and integration progress into launch-ready share-loop and asset packaging decisions once readiness artifacts are closer to complete.
streams.next: Sequence launch asset packaging and share-loop work behind integration confidence and readiness-ledger progress.

streams.name: dashboard/repo readiness
streams.progress: baseline accepted; readiness ledger active; dependency chain made explicit; standards metadata provisional
streams.summary: Repo readiness is primarily a handoff and visibility problem, not a raw setup problem. The dependency chain is explicit, and COO visibility now also notes that standards/compliance framing tied to operating-health references is provisional until archivist confirms the canonical source.
evidence: COO sequencing note: repo readiness is primarily a handoff/visibility problem.
Expected dependency chain is backend canonical contract/package dependency -> frontend contract wiring/integration -> push-readiness artifacts.
COO directed pulse to mark standards/compliance metadata as provisional pending archivist verification.
Archivist accepted archive/push-readiness-checklist.md as the official current push-readiness ledger.
streams.nextMilestoneOrDecisionGate: Verify the canonical operating-health reference with archivist while tracking the readiness dependency chain through integration and artifact completion.

dependencyChain.name: backend canonical contract/package dependency
dependencyChain.note: Backend remains contract authority and defines the canonical v1 slice contract.

dependencyChain.name: frontend contract wiring/integration
dependencyChain.note: Frontend should replace placeholder endpoint handling with live contract-backed integration.

dependencyChain.name: push-readiness artifacts and packaging
dependencyChain.note: Use the accepted readiness ledger to track remaining repo/deploy/package requirements after integration confidence improves.

substreams.name: platform/devops
substreams.blocker: artifact scope/ownership gaps remain for some pipeline items

streams.next: Track the explicit dependency chain through the readiness ledger and surface any ownership or handoff break before push packaging.

decisionGates.name: Core flow baseline approval
decisionGates.summary: Current frontend/core flow is approved and locked as the implementation baseline.

decisionGates.name: Frontend-to-backend contract wiring
decisionGates.summary: Primary execution gate: implement live contract-backed integration against the locked core flow.

decisionGates.name: Push-readiness artifact convergence
decisionGates.summary: After integration advances, complete the remaining repo/deploy/package artifacts tracked in the official readiness ledger.

decisionGates.name: Post-implementation UX refinement pass
decisionGates.summary: Refinement is allowed after implementation progress, but changes to onboarding, matchup logic, or results meaning remain gated.
